violations.text: Inspector observed that locked doors on dumpster had been forcibly removed. PIC worked to replace and re-secure doors during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2025
4 ceiling tiles observed open. Tiles MUST be replaced by 5/5/2025. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, nesting materials, and a deceased mouse. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The door to the outside, located in the stock room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
The dumpster area was observed with trash and debris. Dumpster areas MUST be maintained clean to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The following areas have an accumulation of rodent droppings and/or nesting materials: -under shelving throughout sales floor -under stock in stock room -shelving under balloon area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate as evidenced by product adulteration, rodent droppings, and gnawed packaging. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

Packages of Fiddle Faddle were observed in contact with rodent droppings on a shelf. Packages of dog treats were observed with gnaw marks. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by gnawed packaging, nesting material, and rodent droppings throughout the facility. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of shelving throughout the sales floor have an accumulation of rodent droppings and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The dumpsters were observed overflowing, and the area surrounding the dumpsters (within the enclosure) has an accumulation of trash debris. Dumpster areas MUSt be kept clean to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025
4 ceiling tiles in the salesfloor area were observed missing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025
The floors under the shelving units have an accumulation of dust and rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The presence of cockroaches is not controlled as evidenced by dead roaches within establishment. The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelving have an accumulation of dust and black debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024
The floors in the walk in cooler and mop sink area have an accumulation of dead roaches. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The door to the outsides, located in the front entryway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
Beverages and boxes of snacks were observed stored and displayed on the floor in sales area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The refuse area was observed with an accumulation of litter, leaf litter, and debris. Refuse areas must be maintained free of debris to prohibit harborage of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
